project.title: Matrisome proteins of human and porcine cornea and amnion
project.projectDescription: In this project, for the first time, the Extracellular Matrix protein compositions of porcine cornea and human amnion (amniotic membrane) were  characterized using LC/MS-MS based on an already developed protocol, "Matrisome Project".
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Extracellular matrices (ECMs) produced and assembled by mouse dermal fibroblasts in vitro were decellularized using a decellularization buffer (0.5% Triton-X100, 20 mM ammonium hydroxide) according to previously published protocol (Franco-Barraza, J et al., Curr Protoc Cell Biol, 2016, 71:10.9.1-10.9.34).  ECM proteins were further solubilized using 8M urea and 10mM DTT, deglycosylated and digested into peptides using Lys-C and trypsin following an established protocol (Naba et al., Mol Cell Prot 2012; Naba et al., JoVE, 2015).  Approximately 300 ng of desalted peptides were analyzed at the University of Illinois Mass Spectrometry core facility on a Thermo Fisher Orbitrap Velos Pro coupled with Agilent NanoLC system (Agilent, Santa Clara, CA). The LC columns (15 cm × 75 μm ID, Zorbax 300SB-C18) were purchased from Agilent. Samples were analyzed with a 120-min linear gradient (0–35% acetonitrile with 0.1% formic acid) and data were acquired in a data-dependent manner, in which MS/MS fragmentation was performed on the top 12 intense peaks of every full MS scan. Full MS scans were acquired in the Orbitrap mass analyzer over m/z 400–2000 range with resolution 30,000 (m/z 400). The target value was 1.00E+06. The ten most intense peaks with charge state ≥ 2 were fragmented in the HCD collision cell with normalized collision energy of 30%, these peaks were then excluded for 30 s after 2 counts within a mass window of 10 ppm. Tandem mass spectrum was acquired in the Orbitrap mass analyzer with a resolution of 7,500. The target value was 5.00E+04. The ion selection threshold was 5,000 counts, and the maximum allowed ion accumulation times were 500 ms for full scans and 250 ms for HCD.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: RAW files were converted into. mgf files using MSConvert (ProteoWizard). Database search was carried out using Mascot server version 2.6.2 (from Matrix Science; human database containing 20,365 reviewed entries).  Mascot search parameters were: 10 ppm mass tolerance for precursor ions; 200 mmu for fragment-ion mass tolerance; two missed cleavages of trypsin; fixed modification was carbamidomethylation of cysteine; and variable modifications were oxidized methionine, deamidation of asparagine, pyro-glutamic acid modification at N-terminal glutamine, and hydroxylation of lysine and proline. Only peptides with a Mascot score ≥ 25 and an isolation interference ≤ 30 were included in the data analysis.
